description_long: This structure is a grand historical complex featuring three main pavilions with ornate, blue-tiled, multi-tiered roofs topped by golden finials. The central building is the largest, flanked symmetrically by two smaller pavilions, all connected by raised walkways and surrounded by red and white balustrades. The architectural style is distinctly East Asian, with upturned eaves, intricate wooden framework, and decorative details along the rooflines. The complex is elevated on a stone platform, with broad staircases leading up to the main entrances. The overall layout is highly symmetrical, emphasizing balance and harmony, and the use of vibrant colors and gold accents highlights its ceremonial or imperial significance. The structure is likely constructed from stone, wood, and ceramic tiles, and serves as a prominent cultural or religious landmark.
description: A grand East Asian historical complex with three blue-tiled, gold-topped pavilions connected by raised walkways and ornate red balustrades, set on a stone platform. The symmetrical layout and vibrant colors emphasize its ceremonial and cultural significance.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the three main pavilions, with the central building prominently flanked by two smaller ones and connected by walkways.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the complex, the tiered roofs, and the alignment of the pavilions along the central axis.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the symmetry of the three pavilions and the connecting walkways.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the staircases, raised platform, and the linear arrangement of the buildings.
